title: Integrate modules and run end-to-end chatroom validation
description: Integrate common, server, client, and delivery outputs into one complete course project. Run the approved end-to-end behavior checks and update evidence notes without overstating any manual or limited validation.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN make succeeds THEN server and client build on Linux; WHEN server and at least three clients run THEN login, group chat, private chat, LIST, duplicate nickname rejection, invalid command response, long-message rejection, QUIT, abnormal disconnect cleanup, and server.log recording are validated; WHEN validation cannot run THEN record validation_limited with exact reason; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-chatroom-design.md:320
review_initial_requirements: Verify the four module CSVs are closed or their remaining risks are explicitly recorded before integration begins.
review_regression_requirements: Verify delivered behavior supports all approved acceptance standards, keeps non-goals absent, and produces evidence suitable for the course paper.

title: Review integrated project against approved course-design vision
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare the complete delivered project, evidence, CSV state, validation limits, and review log against the approved design document.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim/evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify INT-01 is closed and module CSV states are consistent before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Check that Linux C++ server and client exist under code/ and build with make; Check that select-based server concurrency and select-based client interaction are actually delivered; Check that group chat, private chat, LIST, duplicate nickname, invalid command, long message, QUIT, abnormal disconnect, and server.log have evidence; Check that final claims distinguish real end-to-end tests from manual, limited, or unrun validation.
